title: pitch-comps output
description: Canonical structured output for the pitch-comps skill. Layer 1 (the data); Layer 2 (rendered comp table) is described in references/rendering.md.

tier.description: Data fidelity: A=paid Stocks Starter+ (unlimited REST, full peer fanout); B=free Basic (rate-limited, curated override only).

tier_caveats.description: Human-readable caveats applicable to the chosen tier.

run_at.description: UTC timestamp when the comp set was built.

enterprise_value.description: USD. EV = market_cap + long_term_debt (cash not subtracted; see references/multiples-methodology.md for the simplification).

peers.description: Peer companies, ordered as the curated map / correlation rank returns them.

data_status.description: full = all multiples computed; partial = some nulls (e.g. negative EBITDA → no EV/EBITDA); empty = financials endpoint returned nothing (often foreign issuer ADRs).

method.description: How the peer set was chosen. See references/peer-selection.md.

n_peers.description: Total peers in the set (data status notwithstanding).

n_peers_with_full_multiples.description: Peers that contributed to summary stats and the regression (data_status == 'full' or 'partial' with at least one multiple).

candidates_considered.description: Total names evaluated before the final peer set was chosen (only meaningful for correlation / SIC methods).

summary.description: Median / mean / 25th / 75th percentile per multiple, computed across peers only (subject excluded). Nulls dropped, not imputed.

regression_adjusted.description: For each multiple, fit `multiple ~ revenue_growth_ttm + ebitda_margin` across peers, predict the subject's implied multiple, and compare to the actual. See references/regression-adjustment.md.

n_peers_used.description: Peers that had non-null values for all controls AND for the dependent multiple. Drives degrees of freedom.

low_n_warning.description: True when n_peers_used < 8; regression is degrees-of-freedom-light and the renderer should flag this.

read.description: One-sentence banker take, generated from the regression-adjusted multiples. Banker-tone, no hedge words.

sources.description: All Massive endpoints called during the run, with fetched-at timestamps for audit.

multiples.description: Trading multiples for one name. Null where the inputs aren't available or the multiple is meaningless (e.g. negative EBITDA).

ev_ebitda.description: Enterprise value / TTM EBITDA. EBITDA = operating_income + D&A. When D&A is null in the financials endpoint (common for software comps), EBITDA falls back to operating_income; the multiple is closer to EV/EBIT in that case. See references/multiples-methodology.md.

p_e.description: Price / TTM diluted EPS. Null when EPS is negative or unavailable.

metrics.description: Underlying metrics used to compute the multiples and feed the regression.

revenue_ttm.description: Sum of revenues from the most recent 4 quarters that reported revenue. USD.

revenue_prior_ttm.description: Sum of revenues from quarters 5-8. Used for the growth calculation.

operating_income_ttm.description: Sum of operating income for the TTM.

depreciation_amortization_ttm.description: TTM D&A from the income statement. Often null for software comps in Massive's endpoint.

ebitda_ttm.description: operating_income_ttm + depreciation_amortization_ttm. Falls back to operating_income_ttm when D&A is null.

diluted_eps_ttm.description: Sum of diluted EPS over the TTM. Null when any quarter is null.

summary_block.description: Summary stats for one multiple across the peer set.

n.description: Number of non-null peer values that fed the summary.

implied.description: Predicted multiple for the subject, given its growth and margin and the peer-fit coefficients.

discount_or_premium.description: (actual / implied) - 1. Negative = subject trades at a discount to peers on this multiple after controlling for growth and margin; positive = premium.

coefficients.description: Fit coefficients on the controls, intercept included.

r_squared.description: Goodness of fit. With n<8 peers this is often artificially high; treat as descriptive.
